core_strengths: Systems-level thinker — naturally maps how components interact before proposing changes
Strong AI implementation instinct: finds where LLMs and automation fit into real workflows, not just theoretically
Analytical depth: approaches problems by understanding root structure before acting
Consulting mindset: translates technical architecture decisions into plain business outcomes
Fast self-directed learner who builds working systems to validate understanding
personality_drivers: Motivated by measurable efficiency gains — not just solving problems, but solving them in ways that scale
Energized by evaluating systems and finding the specific points of friction worth fixing
Intellectually driven by the intersection of AI capabilities and practical organizational constraints
Naturally asks 'what's the architecture here?' before proposing any solution
Competitive drive channeled into performance optimization — in systems and in personal execution
development_areas: Managing tendency to over-engineer when a simpler solution would do
Building patience for ambiguous scopes — consulting often requires acting before full information is available
Balancing technical perfectionism with delivery speed
Actively developing stakeholder communication skills for non-technical audiences

personal_passions.gaming: Strategic and competitive gaming sharpens systems thinking, pattern recognition, and the ability to optimize performance within constraints — directly transferable to consulting and architecture work.
personal_passions.running: Performance-based running reflects the same discipline I bring to technical work: tracking metrics, iterating on approach, and maintaining consistency over the long term.
personal_passions.technology: Continuous hands-on experimentation with AI tools, automation systems, and emerging architectures. I don't just read about new technology — I build with it to understand where it actually works and where it doesn't.
personal_passions.ai_exploration: Actively building AI-powered systems, from RAG pipelines to LLM-integrated workflows, to develop a practical — not theoretical — understanding of what AI can and can't do in production environments.

work_style: Systems-first: understand the architecture before touching anything
AI-augmented: use AI tooling to accelerate every phase of work — research, design, implementation, documentation
Consulting-oriented: always connect technical decisions to business impact
Iterative and experimental: build, test, refine — not plan forever
